item.title: New World Weighs Sale of Stake in $2 Billion HK Hotel Portfolio
item.description: New World Development Co., the cash-strapped property developer, is in talks to sell its 50% stake in a portfolio of three Hong Kong hotels valued at a total of $2 billion, people familiar with the matter said.

item.title: ASG Hospital Said to Plan Filing for $500 Million IPO This Month
item.description: ASG Hospital Pvt., an Indian eye-care chain backed by General Atlantic, is planning to file for an initial public offering as early as this month that could raise up to $500 million, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Alphabet Kicks Off Six-Part Euro Debt Offering
item.description: Google parent company Alphabet Inc. has kicked off the sale of a six-part debt sale in euros, according to a person familiar with the matter who asked not to be identified.

item.title: Swiss Inflation Jumps to Fastest Since 2024 on Iran War Fallout
item.description: Switzerland’s inflation quickened to a 16-month high in April as energy costs jumped because of war in the Middle East.

item.title: Election Pledges in India Risk Wider Deficits, Economists Warn
item.description: Implementing populist promises that featured prominently in election wins in key Indian states this week risk straining their finances, with economists warning of wider fiscal deficits.

item.title: Malaysian Minister Calls for Update on ‘Corporate Mafia’ Probe
item.description: A Malaysian minister urged the country’s police chief to clarify the status of investigations into allegations a network of businessmen was colluding with anti-graft agency officials to oust executives.

item.title: BlackRock Touts ETFs as Liquidity Antidote to Private Exposure
item.description: Exchange-traded funds can be the source of liquidity that retail investors need after ramping up exposure to private assets, BlackRock Inc. executives wrote in a report.

item.title: FTSE 100 Set to Drop on Renewed Iran Tensions
Indian Rupee at Record Low as Analysts Dust Off 2013 RBI Playbook
item.description: The Indian rupee fell to a record low as crude prices rose following a flare-up in violence in the Middle East, leading analysts to consider whether the central bank may return to its 2013 playbook to support the currency.

item.title: Ships Cluster Further From Hormuz Strait as Iran Widens Control
item.description: Hundreds of vessels were seen clustering near Dubai on Tuesday, as more ships moved away from a still-empty Strait of Hormuz in response to Iran’s efforts to widen its area of control.

item.title: RBA Delivers Third Straight Hike, Cementing Outlier Status
item.description: Australia’s central bank raised its key interest rate for a third consecutive meeting, underscoring its determination to tame stubbornly strong inflation and cementing its outlier status among global counterparts.

item.title: Yotta Data Said to Hire Advisors for $900 Million India IPO
item.description: Yotta Data Services Pvt. is weighing an initial public offering in Mumbai that could raise as much as $900 million and has engaged investment banks for the process, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Indonesian Growth Jumps to 5.6%, Beats Estimates Despite War
item.description: Indonesia’s economic growth accelerated in the first three months of the year to the fastest pace since the third quarter of 2022, displaying resilience even as the prolonged war in Iran begins to weigh on global growth.

item.title: Iran Draws a Line and Puts Markets on Notice
item.description: Clash in Gulf sends Brent, inflation expectations to landmark highs.

item.title: Indonesia Intervenes in Markets as Rupiah Sets New Record Low
item.description: Indonesia’s central bank intervened in the foreign-exchange markets as the rupiah set a new record low.

item.title: AI Threatens Private Debt Recovery in Software: Davidson Kempner
item.description: Disruptions caused by artificial intelligence are threatening private credit firms’ potential recovery rates in the software sector, according to Davidson Kempner Capital Management LP chief investment officer Tony Yoseloff.

item.title: Go-Anywhere Bond Funds Stage a Comeback as Flexible Bets Pay Off
item.description: After being “out of vogue” for years, Ariel Bezalel says his bond strategy is back in the spotlight. The fund manager has just wrapped up a tour across South America, where his message was straightforward: in a world of tremendous geopolitical volatility, flexibility pays.

item.title: Star Sports Jumps 204% as Hong Kong Debut Frenzy Extends
item.description: Shares of Star Sports Medicine Co., a maker of medical devices, surged as much as 204% in their Hong Kong trading debut on Tuesday, extending a run of hot first-day performances in the city.

item.title: Apple Explores Using Intel and Samsung to Build Main Device Chips in the US
item.description: Apple Inc. has held exploratory discussions about using Intel Corp. and Samsung Electronics Co. to produce the main processors for its devices, a move that would offer a secondary option beyond longtime partner Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co.

item.title: Oil Spike on Middle East Tensions Puts India’s Stock Rebound at Risk
item.description: Domestic flows, earnings and Modi’s state-election wins could offer support.

item.title: Gemcorp Hires From Oaktree, Deepening Push Into Private Credit
item.description: The emerging markets-focused investment firm Gemcorp Capital Management Limited is turning to Latin America as it expands its private-credit business across developing economies.

item.title: Australian Household Spending Rises at Fastest Pace in Two Years
item.description: Australian household spending rose at its fastest pace in two years in March, driven by a spike in gasoline prices following the outbreak of war in Iran and complicating the Reserve Bank’s efforts to cool the economy.

item.title: Copper Drops as Iran and US Trade Fire in Gulf, Testing Truce
item.description: Copper fell after the US and Iran exchanged fire in the Persian Gulf on Monday, casting doubt over the fate of a ceasefire and raising concerns about further escalation in the conflict that’s upended global markets.

item.title: Philippine Inflation Beats All Estimates at 7.2% Amid War Impact
item.description: Philippine inflation accelerated to the fastest pace in three years, exceeding central bank and analyst projections, underlining the threat to the economy as the Middle East war drives up fuel prices.

item.title: Hedge Fund Firm Aspect Offers China Strategy to Global Investors
item.description: Aspect Capital Ltd. has made one of its China strategies available to international investors for the first time, tapping demand from allocators seeking to diversify their portfolios.

item.title: AI’s Hottest Private Companies Have Booming Crypto Shadow Market
item.description: The race to sell retail investors a piece of the AI boom has gone mainstream — closed-end funds, interval funds, special-purpose vehicles. Now, crypto platforms are offering trades tied to the most valuable private AI companies on earth — ones ordinary investors have almost no other way to access.

item.title: The $1.14 Million Trade That Sparked Hong Kong’s Segantii Insider Trading Trial
item.description: In the closely knit financial community of Hong Kong, major criminal cases are both rare and closely watched.

item.title: Alvarez & Marsal Wants to Make $3.5 Billion From AI Work by 2028
item.description: Alvarez & Marsal wants to generate 50% of its revenue from artificial intelligence work by 2028, which would represent as much as $3.5 billion in earnings.

item.title: ServiceNow Sees $30 Billion Revenue by 2030 on AI Uplift
item.description: ServiceNow Inc. projected it would generate $30 billion of subscription revenue in 2030, attributing the strong outlook to traction from its AI products.

item.title: Gold Holds Losses on Signs That US-Iran Ceasefire Is Fraying
item.description: Gold held a loss as the US and Iran traded fire in the Persian Gulf on Monday, jeopardizing a four-week-old ceasefire and fueling inflation risks.
